metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3741
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This publication provides evidence that Piccolo is involved in presynaptic vesicle clustering. In neuronal cultures of Piccolo knockout mice, with additional knockdown of Bassoon, there is a reduction in the in the number of vesicles per synaptic bouton (Figure 4G-H). There also appears to be a reduction of the number of docked vesicles (Figure 4L, N), but it unclear whether this is a direct effect, or caused indirectly by a reduction in vesicle clustering. Knockout of Piccolo alone had no effect on vesicle clustering or docking, suggesting that Piccolo and Bassoon may be redundant in this.

The data presented in this paper should be considered with some caution. The genetic manipulation to delete Piccolo is a hypomorph, meaning that some Piccolo splice variants are still expressed (Waites et.al. 2011). In addition, due to the very large size of Piccolo, available techniques make it difficult to accurately measure Piccolo levels in cell lysates. Waites et.al. also shows that Piccolo regulates the assembly of presynaptic F-actin, which is in line with phenotype in vesicle clustering.. Experimental description: Mutant mice were generated by homologous recombination experiments with R1 embryonic stem cells and genotyped as described in SI Materials and Methods.
